mentions.note: Enterprise software with extreme P/E shrinkage on generative AI disruption fears; no misses but market psychology negative; avoid until clarity emerges.
HCM and finance software; down 43% but trades 15x despite 18% earnings growth; name-brand quality deeply discounted.
Enterprise software hit by broad AI-driven multiple compression; real earnings impact unclear but valuation pressure is real.
Relief rally today but reported disappointing numbers after close; sell bounces as temporary. Enterprise software under structural attack from AI platforms writing code better and cheaper.
Reported horrible numbers and opened down 12% but recovered nearly 3 points; panic selling created opportunity.

Enterprise software down 23.8% in February; CEO Eschenbach departed, replaced by co-founder Aneel Bhusri; lowered full-year guidance 3 months after prior forecast; now 13x earnings but cohort too hated for bottom-fishing.
New CEO (predecessor of prior CEO) reported disappointment but stock rallied 7% today; AI displacement fears not yet realized in fundamentals.
Enterprise software rebound; believed priced as if going out of business; leadership actively combating AI disruption.
Enterprise software leader in decline as AI upstarts capture value; expensive cloud/SaaS model losing appeal to cheaper AI-native solutions.
Down 40% Q1; double-whammy: enterprise software + HCM focus vulnerable to AI-driven layoffs; CEO stepped down Jan/Feb, mixed quarter—don't try to be hero in this group.
Down 6.5%; caught in SaaS selloff narrative that AI gremlins can duplicate HR/finance software. Narrative existential threat likely overblown but market pricing it in.
Software sector out of favor; quality company suffering market bias against software.
SaaS company rallied 5% in software rotation; uncertainty on sustainability given history of torrential selling after enterprise software rallies.
